mnemonics.title: ACID Properties
mnemonics.content: A.C.I.D. = All Changes In Database - Remember: A (Atomicity - All or Nothing), C (Consistency - Correct state), I (Isolation - Isolated execution), D (Durability - Definitely stored)

acronyms.usage: The four fundamental properties that every transaction must satisfy in DBMS
Concurrency control protocol using growing and shrinking phases
Operations on database items that determine conflicts
A variant of 2PL that releases locks only after commit or abort

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle with arrows showing: Active → Partially Committed → Committed (success path), and Active/Failed → Aborted (failure path). Remember: 'Aborted' always follows 'Failed'.
Draw nodes as circles (T1, T2, T3). Draw directed edges from earlier to later transaction when there's a conflict. NO CYCLE = Serializable. Simple as that!

Think of Read-Write as 'reading someone's diary after they wrote it', Write-Read as 'writing in a diary before someone reads it', Write-Write as 'two people writing in the same diary' - all cause problems!

analogies.analogy: Like a bank transfer - either money leaves Account A AND arrives in Account B, or neither happens. You never want money to disappear into thin air!
Like two people writing in the same Google Doc at different sections - they don't interfere, but the final result is as if one finished completely before the other started.
Like saving a document - once you click 'Save' and it completes, your work persists even if your computer crashes moments later.
Like a restaurant kitchen with multiple chefs - orders get prepared in interleaved ways (chop veggies while sauce simmers), but the final meal is as good as if one chef did everything sequentially.
